behavior_summary: This script implements the object's 'U8P_WTHRONE::look' (examine) handler. When the player examines the wooden throne, the script emits a single short descriptive bark 'wooden throne' and then yields/ends the U8P_WTHRONE::look process.
No branching, menus, flags, or helper functions are present; the behavior is minimal and strictly presentational.
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: show a bark text then call suspend (engine yield/terminate).
No conditional branches or menu/choice logic are present.
open_questions: Does the engine 'suspend' call terminate the process or merely yield it for later resumption? (inferred as end-of-handler; confidence: low)
Are there any external systems that further react to this object's U8P_WTHRONE::look process beyond the visible bark (no evidence in this script; confidence: low)
